item.title: Is a larger down payment worth it in today's mortgage rate environment? What experts say
item.description: A big down payment can make a lot of sense in certain situations, but it's not the right move for homebuyer.

item.title: Lutnick set to testify in House committee's Epstein probe today
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick is scheduled to be questioned Wednesday before the House Oversight Committee as part of its investigation related to Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Driver accused of chasing child on bike down a sidewalk before attempting to break into a home
item.description: A woman in Washington state is accused of trying to run over a child on an e-bike. Police say she later attempted to break into a home. The child was not harmed in the incident and the woman is now facing felony charges.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran strikes, says it's too soon for new direct talks
item.description: Trump threatens Iran with "higher level" strikes if it won't accept a peace deal, but says it's too soon for direct talks after reporting diplomatic progress.

item.title: Investigators seek information on sailboat in connection to missing American in Bahamas
item.description: Authorities are looking for the owners of a sailboat as they continue to investigate the disappearance of Lynette Hooker, the American who went missing in the Bahamas while sailing last month. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Trump administration sends mixed signals on Iran war, Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump is pausing the U.S. mission to guide commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz while questions linger about the future of the war with Iran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: 2 passengers on hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have rare strain, health officials say
item.description: Two passengers who came off a cruise ship infected with hantavirus may have a rare strain that can spread from one human to another, South African health officials say. Tom Hanson has the latest.

item.title: Republicans propose using taxpayer dollars to fund additional ballroom price tag
item.description: President Trump's White House ballroom has a new additional $1 billion price tag and American taxpayers could be covering the costs. Senate Republicans say the taxpayer funds are needed for what they call the "East Wing Modernization Project," including unspecified "above-ground and below-ground security features." Mr. Trump has repeatedly insisted the ballroom would be fully funded with his own money and private donations.

item.title: White House gives mixed messages on Iran war as Trump says Strait of Hormuz operation is paused
item.description: President Trump has paused a U.S. operation in the Strait of Hormuz just two days after it began. The president says it's to try to reach a "complete and final" agreement with Iran. Holly Williams reports.

item.title: Obama appears to criticize Trump in interview with Colbert
item.description: In a taped interview on "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert," former President Obama appeared to criticize President Trump and his administration. Without mentioning Mr. Trump by name, Obama discussed the use of the Justice Department and the expanding powers of the executive branch.

item.title: Eye Opener: Obama appears to criticize Trump, gives stark warning in new interview
item.description: Former President Obama talks to Stephen Colbert and appears to criticize President Trump in a new interview. Plus, new developments in a deadly outbreak aboard a cruise ship. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: 3 evacuated from hantavirus cruise ship as Canary Islands rejects it
item.description: Three passengers were evacuated from the vessel and human-to-human transmission has been confirmed in Switzerland and South Africa.

item.title: Fruit-flavored e-cigarettes for adults OK'd by FDA in major shift under Trump
item.description: The FDA announced its first authorization of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes intended for adult smokers, a major policy shift after months of appeals to President Trump from the vaping industry.

item.title: EEOC sues New York Times, alleging bias against White male worker
item.description: Employment watchdog accuses the New York Times of violating federal law by passing over a White male journalist for a job.

item.title: Employers are demanding AI skills. What's the best way to learn them?
item.description: Career experts say workers and job seekers should take charge of their own AI education. Here's how to get started.

item.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed four people and infected at least four others, officials said.

item.title: 13-year-old student kills 2 women at school in Brazil
item.description: A 13-year-old student admitted carrying out the attack at the public school Instituto Sao Jose in Rio Branco, officials said.

item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire holds for now, Hegseth says, as Trump looks to finalize deal
item.description: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

item.title: Trump largely succeeds in upending Indiana state Senate over redistricting
item.description: President Trump had backed challengers to Indiana state senators who voted against his redistricting plan — and on Tuesday, most of them won.

item.title: Republican Derek Merrin projected to win Ohio's 9th District primary
item.description: Derek Merrin won the GOP primary in Ohio's 9th Congressional District on Tuesday, setting up a rematch against longtime Democratic Rep. Marcy Kaptur in what's expected to be one of the most contested House races of the year.

item.title: 3 killed in U.S. strike on suspected drug boat in eastern Pacific, Pentagon says
item.description: The attack came a day after U.S. forces struck an alleged drug boat in the Caribbean Sea, killing two people.

item.title: Antisemitic graffiti found across Queens, New York
item.description: An investigation is underway in New York City after multiple locations were vandalized with antisemitic graffiti. CBS News' Jared Ochacher reports from Queens.

item.title: Suspect arrested after two fatal Carrollton shootings tied to business dispute, police say
item.description: Police say both shootings were connected and stemmed from a financial dispute between the suspect and the victims.

item.title: Trump pauses Project Freedom in Strait of Hormuz to see if Iran deal can be struck
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he has paused Project Freedom, a U.S. effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: In Lynette Hooker probe, Coast Guard seeks info about mystery sailboat
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is trying to find the owners of a sailboat that may have been moored next to Lynette Hooker's vessel the night she disappeared in the Bahamas, marking a new development in the search for the missing Michigan woman.

item.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
item.description: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

item.title: To avoid risk of mines, Navy directs ships on path farther from Iran
item.description: The U.S. warns that transiting the normal route could be "extremely hazardous" because of mines laid in the Strait of Hormuz by Iran.

item.title: Utz potato chips sold nationwide recalled over salmonella risk
item.description: Utz Quality Foods is recalling some of its Zapp's and Dirty brand potato chips because an ingredient may be contaminated by salmonella.

item.title: U.S. sinks 7 Iranian boats as Iran attacks UAE, ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran launched attacks on the UAE and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz while the U.S. destroyed Iranian boats "that attempted to interfere" with Project Freedom.

item.title: U.S. beginning effort to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the U.S. would "guide" ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz. A U.S.-led task force says it has started the operation.
